commit.message:
fix(i18n): correct mergeLocaleMessage strategy for vue-i18n v9 legacy:false

In vue-i18n v9 with legacy:false, app.config.globalProperties.$i18n is a
stripped proxy object exposing only locale/t/d/n/etc — it has no .global
and no .mergeLocaleMessage.

Fix: accept the host's i18n instance via options.i18n so we can call
options.i18n.global.mergeLocaleMessage() directly. Falls back to legacy
mode ($i18n.global) and fresh install as before.

Host apps must pass { i18n } to app.use(CommunityEngineVue, { i18n }).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(session): auto-restore AI sidebar on Neovim session load (#25)

When a Neovim session is restored (e.g. via `nvim -S Session.vim` or
`:source Session.vim`), aiwaku now automatically reconnects to the
last-used AI tmux session without any user interaction.

The mechanism relies on Neovim's built-in session saving: `mksession`
preserves terminal buffer names. aiwaku names its terminal buffers
`aiwaku://ai-<session-name>`, so on `SessionLoadPost` the plugin can
recover the session name from the saved buffer, verify the tmux session
still exists, and reconnect the sidebar.

- If the sidebar was visible when the session was saved, the same
  window is reused and a live terminal is opened. Focus stays in the
  user's editing window (or terminal insert mode is preserved if the
  sidebar itself was focused).
- If the sidebar was hidden, only `state.current_session` is restored
  so the next `toggle()` reconnects naturally without opening a new
  split.
- If the tmux session no longer exists, a warning is shown and the
  orphaned ghost buffer is cleaned up.

Multiple Neovim instances are safe: each instance restores from its
own session file, which contains its own buffer names, so instances
never pick up each other's sessions.

A new `restore_on_session_load` config option (default `true`) lets
users opt out.

- open_in_new_terminal_buf now accepts a session name parameter and sets
  the buffer name directly via set_buf_name, eliminating the two-step
  intermediate-name approach (tool-N then set_buf_name rename)
- In restore_session, delete the ghost buffer unconditionally before the
  ghost_win check so its session name is freed before open_session or a
  subsequent toggle() tries to claim it, avoiding the E95 name-already-
  in-use error that broke both auto-restore and toggle reconnect
- Replace the nested tabpage/window loop used to find ghost_win with a
  single vim.fn.win_findbuf() call that searches all tabs in one shot
- Remove dead else branch from restore_session (ghost buffer deletion
  is now unified above the if ghost_win check)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add hold-time optimization and IR drop feedback to STA

Task 1: Hold-Time Optimization
- Add HoldFixResult struct to PostRouteOptimizer with metrics:
  num_violations_found, num_fixed, num_buffers_inserted, timing before/after
- Add public fix_hold_violations() method that iteratively:
  1. Runs STA to find hold-violating paths
  2. Inserts delay buffers via DRC-safe placement
  3. Re-checks timing to avoid creating setup violations
  4. Stops when all violations fixed or setup degrades

Task 2: IR Drop Feedback to STA
- Add apply_ir_drop_derating() to StaEngine accepting cell_name->voltage map
- Add set_ir_drop_alpha() and set_ir_drop_nominal_voltage() configuration
- Integrate voltage derating into effective_cell_derate() using alpha-power
  law: delay_scaled = delay_nominal * (V_nominal / V_actual)^alpha
- Default alpha=1.3 (MOSFET velocity saturation model)
- Zero-impact on existing code paths when no IR drop map is set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add latch-based sequential timing and multi-supply-domain timing to STA engine

Latch timing support:
- LatchTimingInfo struct with opening/closing edge, borrow limit, borrowed time
- set_latch_timing_enabled() / set_latch_info() API for opt-in latch analysis
- find_latches() / compute_latch_edges() helpers
- Latch setup check: data_arrival + setup_time <= closing_edge
- Latch hold check: data_arrival >= opening_edge + hold_time
- Time borrowing: surplus slack at latch endpoint lent to downstream path
- DLATCH gates integrated into timing graph, forward/backward propagation,
  gate depth computation, and path extraction

Multi-supply-domain timing:
- VoltageDomain struct and set_voltage_domains() / set_cell_domain() API
- Voltage-dependent delay scaling: delay × (V_ref/V_domain)^alpha (alpha≈1.3)
- Level-shifter delay penalty when timing path crosses voltage domains
  (configurable via set_level_shifter_delay(), default 0.1ns)
- voltage_delay_scale() and level_shifter_penalty() helpers
- Scaling applied to both Liberty-based and default delay models

Both features are opt-in and do not affect existing timing analysis behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SpatialIndex and negotiated congestion routing

- Add SpatialIndex class to physical.hpp/cpp: uniform-grid spatial hash
  supporting insert, remove, query (region + point), rebuild. Handles 1M+
  entries with O(sqrt(N)) query performance.

- Add standalone CongestionMap struct to global_router.hpp/cpp: tracks
  per-gcell usage, capacity, and historical congestion penalties with
  init, congestion_cost, update_history, is_overflowed, total_overflow.

- Add route_negotiated() to GlobalRouter: PathFinder-style iterative
  rip-up-and-reroute with increasing history penalties. Converges when
  overflow reaches zero or max iterations exhausted.

commit.message:
Fix Lagrangian relaxation convergence in tech mapper

Replace the broken lambda update rule (lambda += step * (0.5 - lambda)) which
always damped toward 0.5 regardless of area/delay balance, with a proper
subgradient descent:

- Derive total area and estimated delay from the cost decomposition
- Normalize violations against first-iteration reference values
- Update lambda via subgradient: lambda += step * (area_violation - delay_violation)
- Clamp lambda to [0.01, 0.99]
- Adaptive step size: halve when cost stalls for 2 consecutive iterations
- Early convergence exit when relative cost change < 1e-4 for 2 rounds

commit.message:
fix(wrappers): add config size guard before alloc() in all language wrappers

Add MAX_CONFIG_SIZE (100MB) constant and pre-allocation check in updateState
for Java, Go, .NET, and JS wrappers. This prevents unnecessary WASM memory
allocation and copy for oversized configs — the Rust core enforces the same
limit, but previously the host would allocate+copy the full payload first.

Also add null-check after alloc() in the JS wrapper: if WASM returns ptr=0
(allocation failure), the previous code would silently write to offset 0 of
linear memory, corrupting the WASM heap.

See #27 for a follow-up to make these limits configurable.

Signed-off-by: Simon Schrottner <simon.schrottner@dynatrace.com>

commit.message:
fix: move FMP API key from URL query params to Authorization header

API keys passed as ?apikey= query parameters are exposed in server logs,
proxy logs, browser history, and HTTP caches. FMP supports the apikey
header natively, which keeps credentials out of URLs entirely.

Changes:
- Session-based clients (9 fmp_client.py files): set header on Session
  in __init__ via session.headers.update({'apikey': self.api_key})
- Direct requests.get() callers (6 files): removed apikey from params,
  added headers={'apikey': api_key} to each call
- URL f-string concatenation (representative_stock_selector.py): removed
  ?apikey= from URL, added headers to requests.get()
- etf_scanner.py: removed apikey from params dict, added headers to
  _requests_lib.get(), updated docstring examples
- examples/ copies: same fixes applied to earnings-calendar and
  economic-calendar-fetcher example copies

All 1673 tests pass.

commit.message:
[kernel] B: Fix zombie loss on exit

In RunningProcess::exit_thread(), the zombie thread list was consumed
at line 272 via self.zombie.take() to build zombie_threads. However,
the interrupted-threads branch at line 297 called self.zombie.take()
again, which always returned None because self.zombie was already
consumed. This silently dropped all zombie threads, causing subsequent
join_thread() calls to fail with "thread not found".

Fix: pass Some(zombie_threads) instead of self.zombie.take() in the
interrupted-threads branch, matching the pattern used by exit() and
the other branches of exit_thread().

Add zombie_join_pressure regression test that spawns workers sleeping
simultaneously so the first to wake exits while siblings are still
interrupted, exercising the buggy branch. The test fails without the
fix and passes with it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
